name: Scallops W/Pine Nuts and Dried Tomato
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Bay scallops, or large sea scallops, halved
1/2 cup Oil-packed sun-dried tomatoes, drained and patted dry with paper towels
recipeInstructions:
1. If the dried tomatoes are packed in oil, drain them and pat dry with a towel.
2. Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat.
3. When the oil is almost smoking, toss in the scallops and cook for 30 seconds without stirring.
4. Stir with a wooden spoon and let cook another minute. Using a slotted spoon, remove the scallops to a plate. They should be only partially cooked. Discard the fat from the skillet.
5. Return the skillet to high heat, add the shallots, garlic, wine, lemon juice, and dried tomatoes. Boil, reducing wine by half.
6. Meanwhile, the scallops will have released some liquid. Pour this into the pan and reduce until thick.
7. Return the scallops to the pan, then add the pine nuts. Reduce heat to low and whisk in the butter, adding the chopped parsley, salt, and pepper.
8. Pour onto a serving platter.
